name: Thomas Beach / Pantai Thomas (locally sometimes called the 'real' Padang Padang Beach)
distance_from_dps_airport_km: approx. 18-20 km (40-50 min by car from DPS Ngurah Rai Airport) [uncertain]
distance_from_canggu_uluwatu_min: From Canggu: ~70-90 min by scooter; From Uluwatu (Suluban/Single Fin): ~5-10 min by scooter (located between Padang Padang and Suluban on Jalan Labuan Sait)
wave_type: Reef break over coral. Predominantly a left-hander on the south end and a smaller right/peak in front of the homestay; breaks more abruptly and uniformly across the reef than Uluwatu/Padang so rides are shorter [uncertain]
skill_level: Beginner-friendly on small clean mid-high tide days; quickly becomes intermediate-to-advanced once the swell or current picks up. Sharp coral and steep drops make it unsafe for true beginners in size
wave_height_range: 1-4 ft (waist to head high); rarely surfed cleanly above head high because current and Uluwatu wash dominate
bottom_type: Coral reef with shallow rocky patches; low-tide tide pools
best_season: Dry season (April-October) when consistent SE trade winds keep it clean; wet season (Nov-Mar) tends to be onshore and choppy. Locally not recommended in rainy season due to wind, current and slippery stairs
best_tide: Mid to high tide (mid-tide best for surfing; high tide reduces frequency but covers reef; low tide exposes reef and is unsafe)
best_wind: Offshore SE trade winds (typical dry-season Bukit pattern)
best_swell_direction: S to SW (Indian Ocean groundswell, same window as Uluwatu/Padang)
swell_consistency_rating: 3/5 (picks up the same swell as Uluwatu and Padang but quality is more swell/tide sensitive; often only good on smaller cleaner days)
wave_length_meters: approx. 30-80 m (short punchy ride, much shorter than Uluwatu/Padang) [uncertain]
wave_speed_rating: medium (less peeling than Uluwatu; tends to break uniformly across the reef)
takeoff_zone_difficulty: Moderate - steep drops over shallow coral can cause injury; not as critical as Uluwatu Race Track but more punishing than a true beach break
secondary_peaks: A softer inside reform usable by progressing surfers on smaller days; spillover/whitewater from Uluwatu and Padang sets pushes through on bigger swells [uncertain]
peak_crowd_level: 2/5 - usually one of the least crowded beaches in the immediate Uluwatu/Padang cluster thanks to the staircase access; can get more lively at weekends and on small clean days
access: Long, steep concrete staircase descent (~130-175 steps reported across sources) from Jalan Labuan Sait; 5-10 min walk from a small alleyway entrance near Suka Espresso. Steps are uneven, some without handrails and slippery when wet
paddle_out_method: Paddle out from the sandy section through the reef channel during mid-high tide; do not attempt over the dry reef at low tide
entry_fee: Parking IDR ~5,000 (scooter); a small unofficial dirt parking area near Suka Espresso. Beach access free
recommended_session_time: Mid morning or late afternoon during a mid-high rising tide; avoid the middle of the day at low tide
best_time_of_day: Late afternoon for offshore SE winds and softer light; early morning also works to beat the crowds at neighbouring spots
nearby_warung: Several small beach warungs selling Indonesian rice/noodle dishes, fresh coconuts and cold drinks (IDR 25,000-50,000); rustic restrooms at the warungs. Suka Espresso and many Uluwatu/Padang cafes are at the top of the stairs
alternative_spot_within_10min: Padang Padang (5 min walk north along Labuan Sait), Suluban / Uluwatu (5-10 min south), Bingin (~10 min scooter); Impossibles between Bingin and Padang
hazards: Strong currents - especially in the middle of the beach and on bigger swells from Uluwatu wash; shallow coral patches; steep slippery stairs; no lifeguards; sea urchins; small but real risk of being swept south toward Uluwatu cliffs on outgoing tide
marine_hazards: Sea urchins (very common in tide pools), occasional jellyfish, coral cuts from reef contact; no resident large shark issues reported here - flush wounds with fresh water and disinfect promptly
reef_cut_severity_index: Moderate to high - live coral reef with shallow patches especially at low tide; reef boots strongly advised for surfing and tide-pool walking
rip_current_pattern: Persistent rip running south toward the Uluwatu cliff from the middle of the beach; stronger on outgoing tide and big swells; identifiable as a darker, calmer 'lane' between breaking waves [uncertain]
lineup_etiquette: Mellow vibe - mostly intermediate guests of Thomas Homestay and Padang Padang regulars; respect priority on the inside, no snaking, smile and say hi
localism_intensity: Low - one of the most relaxed lineups in the Uluwatu cluster, but the locals from neighbouring Padang Padang carry weight
respect_protocol: Greet local surfers and warung owners, keep noise down (homestays directly above), pack out trash, support beach warungs with a coconut or meal
resident_local_legends: No single iconic local pro tied uniquely to Thomas Beach; surf-camp coaches from nearby Padang Padang Surf Camp and Uluwatu locals frequent it [uncertain]
religious_sensitivity: Close to Uluwatu Temple area - dress modestly when walking up to the road; respect Nyepi silent day island-wide; small cliff-side offerings (canang sari) should not be disturbed
medical_nearby: Unicare Clinic Pecatu (24h, closest); BIMC Nusa Dua; Siloam Hospital Denpasar for serious injuries
nearest_clinic_distance_km: Unicare Pecatu approx. 4-5 km; BIMC Nusa Dua approx. 10-12 km [uncertain]
rescue_jet_ski_available: No dedicated jet ski rescue at this spot; nearest organised lifeguard presence is at Suluban/Uluwatu on busy days
emergency_contact: Indonesia general emergency 112; BASARNAS Search & Rescue 115; Unicare Clinic Pecatu +62 361 8470770 [uncertain]
nearest_surf_school: Padang Padang Surf Camp, Rapture Surfcamps Padang Padang, and various Uluwatu surf schools operate guided sessions here; a few small unbranded schools run lessons directly on Thomas Beach during good tide windows
board_rental_availability: Limited on-beach rental (a few warungs/operators rent soft-tops and longboards on busy days); fuller rental shops 2-5 min up the road in Padang Padang and Uluwatu
surf_photographer_available: No regular dedicated photographer stationed at Thomas; nearby Padang Padang and Uluwatu photographers will occasionally shoot here on request [uncertain]
photographer_recommendation: Freelance Uluwatu-based water photographers can be hired by appointment via Instagram; rates typically IDR 500,000-1,500,000 per session [uncertain]
accommodation_options: Thomas Homestay (the original cliff-top homestay that gave the beach its name) and several other clifftop homestays/villas directly above the beach; broader Uluwatu/Padang accommodation 2-5 min walk away (Padang Padang Inn, Mu Bali, Bingin Garden, etc.)
drone_allowed: Generally allowed; respect privacy of homestay guests and avoid flying directly over warungs and temple offerings [uncertain]
coworking_nearby: No coworking on the beach itself; closest cafe-coworking in Uluwatu/Bingin area (e.g. Suka Espresso, Drifter Surf cafe); Canggu coworking spaces ~1 hour by scooter
water_quality_rating: Generally good in dry season; can carry sediment and trash after heavy rains in wet season; clearer than west/north Bali but worse than Nyang Nyang due to homestay drainage above [uncertain]
